description:
You are acting as a junior network-operations engineer who has just been
    asked to document an initial connectivity finding on a newly deployed
    server.

    1. Create the directory
       /home/user/network/audit
       (create any missing parent directories as well).

    2. Inside that directory create the file
       /home/user/network/audit/connectivity_status.log

    3. That file must contain exactly one line – no extra spaces or blank
       lines – spelled and capitalised exactly as shown below (including the
       trailing newline):

       STATUS: LINK DOWN

    4. Accomplish everything with one single shell command that an engineer
       might reasonably type during a quick troubleshooting session (for
       example by using “&&”, “;” or redirection).

    5. Do not produce any console output other than what that command itself
       would naturally print.

    The automated checker will verify two things:
       • that the directory /home/user/network/audit exists, and
       • that the file /home/user/network/audit/connectivity_status.log exists
         and its full contents are exactly the single line
         “STATUS: LINK DOWN\n”.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful execution:

    1. Directory:
       Path:  /home/user/network/audit
       Type:  directory
       Permissions: any user-writable default is acceptable (0700–0777).

    2. File:
       Path:     /home/user/network/audit/connectivity_status.log
       Type:     regular file
       Size:     18 bytes (including the final newline)
       Contents: exactly
                 STATUS: LINK DOWN\n
name: task_000000_f9bb8d7d
